commit.message:
Support deletes via a separate flow for tables that support the new ingestion type "cdc_with_deletes". (#33)

Introduces a new **cdc_with_deletes** ingestion type that enables
automatic ingestion of deleted records from source systems.
When enabled, a second streaming flow fetches deleted records and
applies them as deletes to the destination table using apply_as_deletes.

Implements read_table_deletes for HubSpot connector to fetch archived
records. Added tests for the same as well.




CDC with Deletes Architecture

                              ┌──────────────────┐
                              │   Source Table   │
                              └────────┬─────────┘
                                       │
                 ┌─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┐
                 │                                           │
                 ▼                                           ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ read_table() │ │ read_table_deletes() │
│ │ │ │
│ Returns: active │ │ Returns: deleted │
│ records with changes │ │ records (PKs + cursor) │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                ▼                                         ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ {table}_staging │ │ {table}_delete_staging │
│ (view) │ │ (view) │
│ │ │ option: isDeleteFlow=true │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                ▼                                         ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ apply_changes() │ │ apply_changes() │
│ │ │ │
│ Upsert logic │ │ apply_as_deletes=true │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                └─────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
                    ┌──────────────────────────┐
                    │    Destination Table     │
                    │    (Streaming Table)     │
                    └──────────────────────────┘

verification.payload:
tree a1fc4e7f536dcd3103a72056f3f5b66a49f3994a
parent 2e5547fa0403f5b97bb91e2d2b5d3076d84ec263
author Sheldon Tauro <redacted@example.com> 1767807024 -0800
committer GitHub <redacted@example.com> 1767807024 -0800

Support deletes via a separate flow for tables that support the new ingestion type "cdc_with_deletes". (#33)

Introduces a new **cdc_with_deletes** ingestion type that enables
automatic ingestion of deleted records from source systems.
When enabled, a second streaming flow fetches deleted records and
applies them as deletes to the destination table using apply_as_deletes.

Implements read_table_deletes for HubSpot connector to fetch archived
records. Added tests for the same as well.




CDC with Deletes Architecture

                              ┌──────────────────┐
                              │   Source Table   │
                              └────────┬─────────┘
                                       │
                 ┌─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┐
                 │                                           │
                 ▼                                           ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ read_table() │ │ read_table_deletes() │
│ │ │ │
│ Returns: active │ │ Returns: deleted │
│ records with changes │ │ records (PKs + cursor) │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                ▼                                         ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ {table}_staging │ │ {table}_delete_staging │
│ (view) │ │ (view) │
│ │ │ option: isDeleteFlow=true │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                ▼                                         ▼
┌────────────────────────┐ ┌────────────────────────────┐
│ apply_changes() │ │ apply_changes() │
│ │ │ │
│ Upsert logic │ │ apply_as_deletes=true │
└───────────┬────────────┘ └─────────────┬──────────────┘
                │                                         │
                └─────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  ▼
                    ┌──────────────────────────┐
                    │    Destination Table     │
                    │    (Streaming Table)     │
                    └──────────────────────────┘

commit.message:
Propagate table configurations for reading table metadata  (#34)

The `read_table_metadata` of `LakeflowConnect` includes `table_options`
as an input argument but it was never hooked up as the metadata read is
combined as one query for a list of tables.

This PR wraps all table configurations into one JSON and passed into the
Lakeflow Python Data Source and then parse it and use the config for
each table.

verification.payload:
tree 6e6ed5a75e78c5d743fcee9568eadf74cf42827c
parent 591ac39b5f4b279ddcabcc21236dbd2b0d8e87a3
author Yong Li <redacted@example.com> 1767740618 -0800
committer GitHub <redacted@example.com> 1767740618 -0800

Propagate table configurations for reading table metadata  (#34)

The `read_table_metadata` of `LakeflowConnect` includes `table_options`
as an input argument but it was never hooked up as the metadata read is
combined as one query for a list of tables.

This PR wraps all table configurations into one JSON and passed into the
Lakeflow Python Data Source and then parse it and use the config for
each table.

commit.message:
Add delete write-back testing for tables with cdc_with_deletes ingestion type (#37)

1. Added **list_deletable_tables()** and **delete_rows()** methods to
the test utils interface, with HubSpot implementation that archives
contacts and verifies they appear in read_table_deletes.
2. Added two new test cases to the generic test suite:
**test_list_deletable_tables** (validates tables have cdc_with_deletes
ingestion type) and **test_delete_and_read_deletes** (end-to-end delete
→ read verification).
3.  Updated documentation with Step 4 for implementing delete testing.
